title: Legacy001 F1 domain feature micro-family
summary: Apply one compact F1-domain feature family to the RealMLP legacy-001 baseline instead of broad feature expansion.
rationale: The legacy-001 baseline already has useful ratios, lap-time/degradation interactions, count encodings, bins, and two combo target encodings. A narrow patch that adds race-relative lap-time and tyre-context features can test whether the model benefits from richer F1 strategy signals without confounding the result with a large feature rewrite.
implementation_hint: Patch the parent feature engineering only. Add a small, coherent feature family such as TyreLife/RaceProgress, TyreLife*RaceProgress, LapNumber-TyreLife, Cumulative_Degradation/TyreLife, LapTime delta versus Race median, and LapTime rank within Race. Preserve the parent RealMLP training loop, folds, target encoding strategy, output files, and validation protocol.
expected_effect: Improve OOF AUC if pit-stop risk is better represented by tyre age, degradation, and local race pace context than by the current raw and simple interaction features alone.
risk: Race-level covariate statistics must not use the target or any prediction-derived signal; adding too many correlated features could add noise or overfit.
